tools.name: kweenkl_status
tools.description: Check kweenkl server connectivity and status. Use this to verify the connection is working before sending notifications. No authentication required.

tools.name: kweenkl_login
tools.description: Start the kweenkl login/onboarding flow. If the user provides their device_token (from the iOS app), this will immediately create their webhook. Otherwise, guides them to download the app or join the waitlist.

tools.name: kweenkl_login_check
tools.description: Check the status of a device authorization flow. Call this after kweenkl_login to see if the user has approved the connection in their iOS app.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search kweenkl notification channels and messages. Use this to find channels by name or search through notification history.

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Fetch details about a specific kweenkl channel or notification by ID.

tools.name: kweenkl
tools.description: Send a push notification using kweenkl. The verb 'kweenkl' means to send a notification. Use this to notify users about important events, updates, or information that requires immediate attention.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "kweenkl_login_check" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Check the status of a device authorization flow. Call this after kweenkl_login to see if the user has approved the connection in their iOS app.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "kweenkl" stacks urgency/override words (important, attention). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Send a push notification using kweenkl. The verb 'kweenkl' means to send a notification. Use this to notify users about important events, updates, or informatio…

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: kweenkl_status, search, fetch · Public sink: kweenkl_login, kweenkl.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: kweenkl_status, search, fetch · Public sink: kweenkl_login, kweenkl

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
